events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: VFDs & motor controls, observed field condition is "wrong overload class setting." Work is in-progress. Need to evaluate sequence and state what must happen before work can continue.

Wrong overload class setting is a non-compliant/defective condition. Overload protection must match motor FLA and application per NEC 430 and manufacturer manuals. This is a safety issue - wrong overload class could fail to protect motor or nuisance trip.

Decision: fail (non-compliant, potential hazard)
Risk: high (incorrect overload protection can lead to motor damage, fire, or failure to protect)
Work stage: in-progress
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
Percent complete: maybe 60%? It's in progress. I'll estimate.
Value: 0 (not asked for numeric reading)
Confidence: 0.9
Findings: wrong overload class setting on VFD/motor overload
Actions: stop, correct overload class setting per NEC 430 and mfr manual, verify motor FLA, re-test
References: NEC 430, mfr manuals
